rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type mismatches and invalid references in implementation scope contracts.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible AADL features or declared helpers to avoid '<error>' type issues.
When AGREE reports scalar type resolution errors, use correct AGREE types: int, real, bool.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always reference only visible identifiers from the current scope to prevent type resolution failures.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings or declared auxiliary variables.
Avoid placing implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes; move to implementation scope instead.
When diagnostics point to invalid expressions, trace root causes like unresolved identifiers or incorrect types and fix them directly.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid AADL scopes.
AGREE scalar type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or unknown types.
Left-hand side of assign statements must be an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; move to implementation scope or remove.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by adding new architecture; instead, resolve root causes in existing visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes for implementation-level bindings
